revenue: $17.60 billion, up 53.9% YoY from $11.44 billion in Q3 2024
net_income: $5.58 billion, up 475.3% YoY from $970.3 million in Q3 2024
key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $6.21, up from $1.07 in Q3 2024
Research and development expenses of $3.47 billion, up from $2.73 billion in Q3 2024
Acquired in-process research and development (IPR&D) decreased to $655.7 million from $2.83 billion in Q3 2024
qoq_changes: Cash and cash equivalents increased significantly to $9.79 billion from $3.27 billion at year-end 2024
Inventories grew to $12.18 billion from $7.59 billion at year-end 2024, indicating a ramp-up in supply
Long-term debt increased to $40.87 billion from $28.53 billion at year-end 2024
new_risks: Risks from the proliferation of counterfeit, misbranded, adulterated, or illegally compounded products
Risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence or other emerging technologies exacerbating competitive, regulatory, and cybersecurity vulnerabilities
Issues with product supply stemming from manufacturing difficulties, unpredictability in demand, and labor shortages
management_tone: Bullish, driven by explosive 54% top-line growth and massive net income expansion reflecting strong product demand and favorable operational leverage.
analyst_note: Eli Lilly delivered a phenomenal Q3 2025, with revenue reaching $17.6 billion and net income surging over 470% YoY due to lower IPR&D costs and robust sales. The massive inventory build and increased cash position suggest the company is aggressively scaling supply to meet the unprecedented demand for its key therapeutics.
